headline: Ethiopia 24-hour override: TPLF reinstated pre-coup Tigray regional council April 20-28, a move Tigray Interim President Tadesse Worede declared 'nullifies the Pretoria Agreement.' EU statement May 1 said it was 'imperative' that 'another devastating conflict is avoided in Tigray.' Three US Embassy diplomats traveled to Mekelle to meet TPLF leaders. The TPLF move is a sub-national party action that the federal government has declared illegitimate; Ethiopia (federal) is responding with diplomatic engagement and EU coordination. Floor-proximate but not floor-designated. Net assessment: peace-accord stability erosion (regardless of which party at fault) is a modest BND signal; federal government's diplomatic posture and EU engagement are mildly positive. Composite 5.9 (published) → 5.6 (-0.3). Sub-threshold delta. Critical band sustained. Floor-proximity sustained but no floor designation triggered. Recommend confirm with notation.
key_evidence: April 20-28 2026: TPLF reinstated pre-coup Tigray regional council from 2020 election — federal government and Tigray Interim Administration declare illegitimate.
April 22-28 2026: Tigray Interim President Lt. Gen. Tadesse Worede stated the TPLF move 'nullifies the Pretoria Agreement.'
April 9 2026: Federal Council of Ministers extended Tadesse Worede's TIA mandate one year — TPLF action came AFTER this extension.
May 1 2026: EU statement: 'imperative that another devastating conflict is avoided in Tigray.'
Three US Embassy diplomats traveled to Mekelle to meet TPLF leaders (diplomatic intervention).
Just Security analysis: Ethiopia is at a 'crossroads.'

Cumulative baseline sustained: Tigray hunger crisis (1,300+ deaths over 3 years from hunger and lack of medicine per Al Jazeera Jan 2026); USAID cuts impact; HRW 2026 World Report on Amhara/Oromia hostilities.

evidence.finding: TPLF restores Tigray government; fears for Pretoria Agreement.
EU: imperative that another devastating conflict is avoided in Tigray.
Tigray Interim President: TPLF move nullifies Pretoria Agreement.
Ethiopia at a 'crossroads' in Tigray peace process.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 unchanged. Federal government and EU acknowledged escalation risk; awareness mechanism functioning at floor-proximate level.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.5 unchanged. Federal diplomatic engagement (TIA mandate extension; EU coordination; US Embassy outreach) is responsive but not preventive.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 → 1.4 (-0.1). B3 Scope Clarity: peace-accord stability is the central boundary instrument and Pretoria Agreement integrity is now formally questioned by Tigray Interim Administration. Even though TPLF is a sub-national actor, the federal-state inability to prevent or rapidly de-escalate the unilateral move is a state-level boundary signal. Modest dock; not floor-level because federal-state engagement remains functional.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.5 unchanged. Federalist structure under stress but operating; constitutional framework intact.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 unchanged. Federal government's stated commitment to Pretoria Agreement is being tested by sub-national party action; no federal-state inconsistency in window.

math_check.composite_rounded_post_published_compare: Note: published composite 5.9 reflects different subdimension baseline (likely earlier scoring snapshot in countries.json — AWR/EMP/ACT/BND/ACC/SYS/INT all at 1.5, EQU at 1.0). Reconstructed composite from those means is ((1.4375-1)/4)*100 = 10.94. Published 5.9 vs. reconstructed 10.9 is a math-hygiene discrepancy of -5.0; this matches the floor-proximate-cluster compositing pattern.
math_check.validator_check: Math-hygiene flag noted: published 5.9 cannot reconstruct from 1.5/1.0 dimension means. Per scanner rules, math-hygiene cluster issues are flagged for data-team review only — no score change proposed beyond the BND -0.1 dock. Recommend confirm + math-hygiene flag escalation.

math_hygiene_flag.issue: Published composite 5.9 cannot reconstruct from current published dimension means (AWR/EMP/ACT/BND/ACC/SYS/INT 1.5; EQU 1.0). Reconstructed composite is 10.9. Discrepancy: -5.0 points.
math_hygiene_flag.action: Flag for data-team review per math-hygiene cluster protocol. Do NOT propose score change to address discrepancy in this cycle.
math_hygiene_flag.score_proposal_action: Apply only the BND -0.1 evidence-driven dock; defer composite-reconstruction question to data-team.

negativeSignals: TPLF reinstated pre-coup council; Pretoria Agreement integrity formally questioned by TIA; conflict-risk re-elevated; cumulative Tigray hunger crisis sustained.
positiveSignals: Federal government extended TIA mandate (institutional continuity); EU diplomatic engagement; US Embassy outreach; Tadesse public framing of TPLF move as illegitimate.
sector_context: Conflict-floor cluster proximity sustained. Ethiopia is floor-proximate but NOT floor-designated (federal institutions still functioning, EU/US engaging diplomatically). The TPLF unilateral move is the most consequential peace-accord stability event since Pretoria signing. Cross-reference: Tigray as a sub-national entity is not separately scored; federal-state-level scoring captures the dynamic.
open_actions_resolved: TPLF reinstatement scored under BND -0.1 dock
Federal/EU/US diplomatic engagement captured under positive signals
Math-hygiene discrepancy flagged for data-team review
open_actions_carried: Monitor whether Pretoria Agreement breach materializes into renewed armed conflict
Watch: TPLF Council president election (planned May/June per Tadesse) — escalation milestone
